schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-21 design conversation: 'looks good to me').
intention: A component's charter factors into two files: a bounded `state` (the current cursor + a WINDOW of recent history) and an append-only `history` (the full voyage log). A PROJECTOR regenerates the window from history on append — the write-path is just: append; the tool shapes the window. This restores the charter's 'true and small by construction' property (the mutable journey stops bloating the file every mind reads first) and makes the bound STRUCTURAL: the window is fixed-size by the projector, not by anyone's discipline (Law 4).
why: Measured 2026-07-21 across four charters: `state` is the single LARGEST field (17–24%) and the most mutable — the charter had become the ticket, and the file every mind reads first grows without bound. Splitting bounds it by physics. It is Law 1 made physical at charter scale: 'what is the recent state' is answered once on append and turned into STRUCTURE (the window), so no mind re-derives it by reading the whole journal — the same move as inference_domain (compile-once), one scale in (Akien: 'it's compiled inference'). `history` append-only = Law 7 native (a record of truth is never mutated, only appended — the shape of db_domain's INSERT-only store). And it places state where class-space allows: the charter beside code stays PURE intention+why (CLAUDE.md: 'code only, no state, ever'), while `state`/`history` live in the knowledge repo where tickets and their state already sit.
traces_to: Law 1 (every answered question becomes structure; re-deriving the settled is a defect — the projector compiles the window so the journal isn't re-read)
Law 7 (history is append-only; a record of truth is never mutated)
Law 4 (the bound is physics — a fixed-window projector — not a discipline to remember)
Law 5 (intent stays at the address — the charter beside code — while the mutable record moves to where state belongs)
CLAUDE.md — the charter's charge to 'stay true and small by construction' (lost as `state` grew; restored here)
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> TICKETME -> BUILDME -> [PROVEME] -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor advanced 2026-07-21: child (a) the schema + projector is BUILT + PROVEN — cairn/charter/projector.py, proofs/test_projector.py green under the tester, VALIDATION persisted via db_domain + grepped back (claim 'proof test_projector.py passes under python3', green, 15:50:30). Placement (b) RESOLVED 2026-07-21 (beside-code git-JSON; database keeps only graph trees; validations move out of db_domain). Still open: migration (c, inference_domain last/by its own ruling), CLAUDE.md/MAP (d), and a spawned sibling to migrate the validations table out of db_domain. The parent is not PROVED until c/d land.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: append a transition to a component's `history`; show the projector regenerates the window BOUNDED (last-n / since-last-gate); show `state` cannot grow unbounded; show the ONLY write is append (single door); show the window never drifts from history's tail. THE STANDING LESSON, made a gate: the VALIDATION must be PERSISTED via db_domain (store.persist_validation) and grep back — a produced-but-unpersisted validation did NOT happen (the miss that bit inference_domain, 2026-07-21). Durability gate: not done until proven, VALIDATION persisted, AND committed (git).
falsifier: the charter stays small BY CONSTRUCTION (`state` bounded, cannot bloat); the common briefing read touches only `state`; `history` is append-only and never rewritten; the window matches history's tail (the projector is faithful — no drift). Wrong-shape signals: (1) if the window is hand-maintained instead of projected, the split reintroduces the drift it was meant to kill — the projector is NOT optional, it is what makes the split honest; (2) if `state` starts carrying anything that is not cursor+window, the charter is becoming the ticket again; (3) if `history` must be edited in place rather than appended, it is not a record of truth (Law 7 breach).
horizon: first real split — when at least one live component runs on `state`+`history` with a projector shaping the window on append, and the charter beside code is pure intention+why (no state). Re-check then: the right window rule (last-n vs since-last-gate — guess and adjust, Law 3), and whether the projector stays trivial or some future window earns the inference-domain cache.
children: (BUILT + PROVEN 2026-07-21) a — the two-file schema + the PROJECTOR: `state` (cursor + window) compiled from append-only `history`. cairn/charter/projector.py — pure core (append/project, storage-agnostic) + a thin file layer (append_entry = the single write-door). Proven green under the tester, 5 teeth (derived-not-authored/no-drift, bounded-by-construction, append-only, since-gate self-sizes, cursor-tracks-head); VALIDATION persisted + grepped back. Window rule DEFAULT_WINDOW=count(5), a guess to adjust (Law 3).

(RESOLVED 2026-07-21, Akien ruling) b — PLACEMENT: `state`/`history` AND `validations` all live BESIDE THE CODE as git-JSON, next to the thing they describe (`state`/`history` beside `intention+why.json`; a VALIDATION beside the `proofs/` it seals). The rule: everything we plan to share goes in the code (git files) EXCEPT the graph trees (embeddings), which go in the database — the database ends up holding ONLY the graph trees (validations were there today; they MOVE out). This REVERSES this child's original filing (which put state/history in the knowledge repo citing 'code only, no state, ever'): that CLAUDE.md line is precisely what this afternoon's work revises — 'no state' means RUNTIME/INSTANCE state (which stays in ~/.cairn/); build-provenance (history AND its sealing validations) is knowledge, frozen at PROVED, and belongs beside the code it explains (Law 5 extended — intent, its voyage, AND its proofs share the address). RIPPLES: (i) db_domain shrinks — it stops owning `validations`, keeps only the graph trees / truly-relational data; a separate ticket migrates the `validations` table out to beside-code git files (the projector's own validation, persisted to db_domain 15:50 today, is the first to move). (ii) The standing-lesson gate baked into this ticket + harbor-master ('VALIDATION persisted via db_domain + grep back') KEEPS ITS LESSON (produced-but-not-durably-recorded didn't happen) but changes its TARGET to a beside-code git validation file. (iii) Unblocks harbor_master's aggregate register (it aggregates over these beside-code git histories). (iv) Makes child (d)'s CLAUDE.md rewrite load-bearing — the 'no state, ever' line must be corrected to say runtime/instance state. THE FULL PARTITION (Akien-agreed 2026-07-21) — intent shares its IMPLEMENTATION's address, read one level up for conceptual work: a CODE-SEAM has code, so it lives in cairn/ with everything beside it (intent, history, validations); a CONCEPT-PIECE's implementation IS the document (the resolved prose), whose home is CairnCommons/ by definition ('if losing it loses knowledge, it's commons'), so its intention + tickets + resolution (a quorum-signature record, not a tester VALIDATION — different hands seal it) all co-locate THERE; the database holds ONLY the graph trees. Partition question: does this intention have code? yes -> cairn/, no -> CairnCommons/. Consequence: a code-seam's ticket STARTS in CairnCommons/tickets/ as staging and MIGRATES beside the code to become its `history` when it resolves (the citadel model's 'the ticket moves to where the code winds up'); a concept-piece ticket never migrates — CairnCommons/ is already its berth, it becomes `history` in place. So CairnCommons/tickets/ is a launch pad for code work but the permanent berth for conceptual work.

(follows) c — MIGRATE existing charters: extract the `state` field from inference_domain / db_domain / system_rackmount / base / ground_loop / bus / etc. into `state`+`history`; the charter beside code becomes pure intention+why. NOTE: inference_domain is FROZEN pending the parked process discussion (2026-07-21) — migrate it last / by its own ruling, not reactively.
(follows) d — update CLAUDE.md's 'co-locates intention+why.json' line + MAP to reflect the split; recompile.
waits_on: nothing for the core (child a). Child b (placement) RESOLVED 2026-07-21 — beside-code git-JSON for state/history/validations, database keeps only the graph trees. Remaining: child c (migrate existing charters, inference_domain last), child d (CLAUDE.md 'no state, ever' -> runtime/instance state + MAP recompile), and a SPAWNED sibling — migrate the `validations` table out of db_domain to beside-code git files.
provenance: cast 2026-07-21 via /sorted, at the close of a design conversation that began as 'the intention+why.json files get large' and converged on: split by the state-vs-history seam; bound the window with a PROJECTOR (Akien: 'it's compiled inference, CC++'); guess the window and adjust (Law 3 + grow-against-need). Measurement grounded it (state = 17–24%, the biggest + most mutable field). Sibling-cast with harbor-master (the fleet-scale aggregate over these local histories). Ratified by Akien: 'looks good to me.'
